title: Split analyst into baby-analyst (Sonnet, BAU-drain, ~2h cadence) + analyst (Opus, deep work, 4h cadence). Class-based model-split pattern, phased deployment, conservative scope on phase 1.
depends_on: PROP-025 (review_class field — the primary routing axis for the split)
PROP-027 (class_hint on issues — secondary routing signal)
PROP-029 (decider writes tracker entry on ROUTE-TO-ANALYST — both baby and analyst dispatch on tracker)
PROP-030 (backlog-trend monitoring — measurement instrument for validating the split's effectiveness)
PROP-031 (decider-bau-route source enum — the work-stream baby is designed to drain)
headline_recommendation: APPROVE phased deployment, NOT full directive-as-stated. Phase 1: baby-analyst on Sonnet at 2h cadence (not hourly), Mode 1 BAU drain ONLY (not Mode 2b, not Mode 1b verification). Phase 2 (conditional on 5-7 days of green PROP-030 metrics): expand baby to Mode 2b attention-inbox sweep. Phase 3 (conditional on Phase 1+2 success): consider 'Mode 1b verification class' carve-out — but only after declaring a `prediction_class` field on predictions.json, because the directive's 'verification-class Mode 1b' is not a pre-existing concept in the codebase. Sequencing rationale lives in §counter_argument and §phased_implementation_plan below.

executive_summary.problem_one_line: analyst dispatcher's strict-priority ordering (Modes 0 > 1 > 1b > 2 > 2b > 3 > 4) means Mode 1 BAU drain rarely wins the dispatcher slot when curmudgeon raises any work — empirically today (2026-05-13) Mode 1 BAU drain fired exactly once across 4 dispatched runs, while 137 pre-PROP-029-orphan ISSs sit at status='assigned-analyst' and the assigned_analyst_count has been stuck at ~151 for 36+ hours despite a HEALTHY net closure velocity (135/7d).
executive_summary.fix_one_line: Decouple BAU drain from Opus-driven deep work by introducing dome-analyst-baby (Sonnet, ~2h cadence, narrow dispatcher: Mode 1 BAU drain only on Phase 1) that reads the SAME expansion-tracker.json + open-issues.json files but is constrained to ONLY pick up items with `source IN {decider-bau-route, decider-m1-route, decider-m3-carry-over}` OR `review_class === 'verification'` OR orphan assigned-analyst ISSs of severity minor/moderate.

tinker_top_line_recommendations.Q1_split_dimension: HYBRID — primary axis is `review_class` (per PROP-025 author-declared, advisory hint from PROP-027 when class is null). Fallback when `review_class` and `class_hint` are both null: `source` enum. Concretely: baby owns items where (a) `review_class === 'verification'`, OR (b) review_class is null AND `source IN {decider-bau-route, decider-m1-route, decider-m3-carry-over}`, OR (c) the item is an orphan assigned-analyst ISS of severity minor/moderate (pre-PROP-029 backlog). analyst owns everything else: `review_class IN {'deep-attack', 'holistic'}`, OR review_class null AND source NOT in the baby-owned set (typical: `analyst-hnote-author`, `analyst-mode1-orphan-drain` self-authored, manual operator-routed). Rationale: review_class is already authoritative per PROP-025 — using it for model routing extends an existing declared-state surface rather than inventing a new one.
tinker_top_line_recommendations.Q2_boundary_cases_curmudgeon_driven_veri: If decider routed an ISS to analyst with class_hint='verification', a tracker entry was written with review_class hint inherited. Baby picks it up. On intake, if baby determines the work is actually deep-attack scope (e.g., the curmudgeon review revealed a new argument needs construction, not just verification), baby's escape-hatch is to ESCALATE: write a small `escalate-to-analyst.json` marker in monitor/analyst/expansions/ with `escalated_from: 'analyst-baby'`, `original_review_class: 'verification'`, `new_review_class: 'deep-attack'`, and the rationale. The marker re-routes the item to analyst's next run by being a tracker entry analyst's Mode 1 dispatcher would pick up. Cost of an escalation is one no-op baby run + one normal analyst run, vs the alternative of having baby produce a low-quality deep-attack EXP. Worth it.
tinker_top_line_recommendations.Q3_coordination_writes_to_tracker: Confirmed: standard PROP-029 multi-writer protection suffices. The existing decider+analyst share-writer protection (a) git pull --rebase at run start, (b) pre-push integrity gate, (c) git merge-conflict detection, (d) non-concurrent scheduling) is symmetric in number-of-writers. Adding a third writer (baby) does not change the protection's correctness — only its frequency of triggering rebases. Expected rebase-conflict rate at the new 3-writer ×~7-12 runs/day cadence: <1 per day, well within current handling capacity. Note: workspace-sync's universal-pusher rescue path (CLAUDE.md §Universal-pusher mode) ALREADY covers `expansion-tracker.json` round-trip from FUSE — no new file needs OWNERSHIP/NEVER_PUSH classification.
tinker_top_line_recommendations.Q4_collision_risk_baby_2h_vs_analyst_4h: Acceptable. Proposed cadence: baby at `20 */2 * * *` (every 2h at :20), analyst unchanged at `50 */4 * * *` (every 4h at :50). Minimum gap between back-to-back baby↔analyst runs: 30 min (e.g., baby at 04:20Z, analyst at 04:50Z). Per-run clones isolate working state. The narrow contention is the tracker-write race: both could pick up the same item if their clones started before the other wrote 'complete'. Mitigation: each agent claims an item by writing `status='in-progress'` + `claimed_by: <agent-name>` + `claimed_at` to the tracker BEFORE doing the work; on re-read, if the item already has `status='in-progress'` or has been moved to `complete`, agent skips it. This is a small dispatcher change (add a claim-stamp step before work). Same pattern decider+analyst already use implicitly via tracker write-then-work ordering.
tinker_top_line_recommendations.Q1_schedule_hourly_vs_2h: 2h cadence, NOT hourly. The directive proposes hourly (`20 */1 * * *`); my analysis shows that's too aggressive. Math: current backlog is 137 orphan ISSs + 14 tracker pending = ~151 drain candidates. At baby's likely throughput of 5-10 ISSs/run, the backlog drains in 15-30 runs. At hourly cadence, that's 15-30 hours of work; at 2h cadence, 30-60 hours. EITHER cadence drains the backlog within 3-4 days. Steady-state throughput need: decider's PROP-031 BAU 3b routes ~5-10 ISSs/day. At 2h cadence (12 runs/day) baby has 12 dispatcher opportunities to drain ~5-10 new items — i.e., 8-11 of 12 runs/day are no-ops in steady state. At hourly (24 runs/day), 19-22 of 24 runs are no-ops. Sonnet no-op runs cost ~$0.01-0.05 each (context-load only), so the cost delta hourly-vs-2h is ~$0.20-0.40/day — minor, but 2h is the closer fit to the actual work-arrival rate and reduces overall context-load attention on a noisy operator-facing latest-baby-summary stream. Recommend 2h. Hourly remains a fallback if 2h proves insufficient.
tinker_top_line_recommendations.Q2_baby_owns_daily_report_or_share: Baby writes its own `monitor/analyst-baby/latest-baby-summary.txt` (new directory, new file) — keeps audit trails per-agent and avoids race-writes against analyst's own summary. Tinker's pipeline-health checks should extend their staleness check to cover this new file. Tradeoff: one more thing to track. Worth it for clarity.
tinker_top_line_recommendations.Q3_share_latest_summary_or_separate: Separate — `latest-baby-summary.txt` lives at `monitor/analyst-baby/latest-baby-summary.txt`, NOT in `monitor/analyst/`. Reason: when an operator-Claude session needs to investigate analyst pipeline health, having the two summaries in different directories makes the agent-distinction obvious in directory listings and `grep`. Operator's morning briefing (latest-decider-summary.txt) can pull from both files.
tinker_top_line_recommendations.Q4_phase1_phase2_phase3_sequencing: See §phased_implementation_plan for the full sequencing logic. Headline: Phase 1 deploys baby with Mode 1 BAU drain only. Phase 2 (after 5-7 days of green PROP-030 metrics) expands to Mode 2b. Phase 3 considers Mode 1b verification class — but only if `prediction_class` field is introduced first (it doesn't exist today).

executive_summary.scope_clarification: This PROP authorizes design only. All edits are agent-applied prompt edits + one new scheduled task creation + one new directory. NONE are NEVER_PUSH (monitor/prompts/*.md is git-owned agent-applied; scheduled task creation is operator-side via Claude desktop UI or scheduled-tasks MCP). Operator reviews PROP, then either tinker self-applies the prompt edits (under PROP-014-amendment-001 Q6 lifecycle) or operator routes via directive.

empirical_validation_today.directive_claim: analyst ran 4 times today (05:06Z, 09:11Z, 13:04Z, 17:07Z); only the LAST run entered Mode 1 BAU drain

tinker_measured.analyst_runs_today: 5 (mtime evidence on EXP outputs: 04:06Z, 08:11Z, 12:04Z, 16:07Z, 20:05Z). Directive count of 4 was filed at 17:35Z, before the 20:05Z run.

exp_outputs_today.EXP-364-SEC-6.14-c3-batch.json: 04:06Z — c3 batch (7 ISSs), CURMUDGEON-driven Mode 1 normal (not BAU drain)
exp_outputs_today.EXP-365-kappa-cluster-c5-batch.json: 08:11Z — c5 batch (4 ISSs), CURMUDGEON-driven Mode 1 normal (not BAU drain)
exp_outputs_today.EXP-366-stale-tracker-drain-batch.json: 16:07Z — STALE-TRACKER DRAIN BATCH (12 ISSs), Mode 1 BAU drain pattern: 'M1 had 27 pending tracker entries; 10 had ISSs already closed in closed-issues.json...'
exp_outputs_today.EXP-326-WIN-021-tau-I-framing-close.json: 20:05Z — c4 singleton verification, curmudgeon-driven (not BAU drain)

tinker_measured.interpretation: Of 5 analyst runs today, 1 (20%) was a Mode 1 BAU drain. Directive's '1-of-4' claim is verified. The pattern is real — BAU drain wins the dispatcher slot ~1-in-4-or-5 runs.

queue_breakdown_for_routing_design.interpretation: 138/151 (91%) have NO class_hint — they're the pre-PROP-029 orphan bolus. 100/151 (66%) are severity=minor; 48/151 (32%) are moderate. Only 2 are major. This is a near-perfect fit for Sonnet baby-analyst BAU drain — large volume of mechanical close-out work on low-severity items.

tracker_state.interpretation: 39 of 61 7d-tracker entries are source=decider-bau-route — this is exactly the work-stream baby is designed to drain. Their review_class is mostly null (decider doesn't always set it), but their source is consistent and unambiguous. Source-fallback routing handles this cleanly.

context_verification.verification_of_directive_premise: Directive's premise (Mode 1 BAU dispatch starvation) is empirically confirmed: 1-of-5 Mode 1 BAU drain wins today, 137 stuck orphans, assigned_analyst_count flat at 151 despite healthy closure rate. PROP-030 metrics already fire OPERATOR_ESCALATION tier this run (assigned_analyst_count > 150). The structural fix in PROP-031 (BAU 3b routing) is feeding the queue at ~5-10 ISSs/day from decider, and analyst's BAU drain can't keep up at 1 successful dispatch per 5 runs.

by_mode.directive_proposal: baby = Mode 1 (BAU drain) + Mode 2b (attention inbox) + Mode 1b (verification class)
by_mode.tinker_evaluation: Partial-approve. Mode 1 BAU drain is the right scope for Phase 1 — it's the empirically-validated bottleneck. Mode 2b (attention inbox) is a natural Phase 2 addition because attention-inbox items are mostly mechanical (per analyst.md's 3-phase OBE/batch/singleton procedure — Phases 1+2 are literally 'mechanical empirical verification', Phase 3 is the substantive one). Phase 1+2 split: baby handles OBE+batch, analyst handles singleton substantive. Mode 1b 'verification class' is NOT a pre-existing concept — predictions.json has no `prediction_class` field. Either (a) we introduce `prediction_class` first (Phase 3 prerequisite), or (b) we accept that Mode 1b stays on Opus for now.

by_review_class.directive_proposal: baby reads tracker for items where item.review_class === 'verification' OR item.source in {'decider-bau-route','decider-m1-route','decider-m3-carry-over'} AND ISS count > 1 (batchable). analyst reads tracker for items where item.review_class IN {'deep-attack','holistic'} OR singleton high-effort items.
by_review_class.tinker_evaluation: Approve with adjustments. The 'AND ISS count > 1 (batchable)' constraint is too restrictive — many decider-bau-route entries are singletons (single ISS = single tracker entry per PROP-029 Q2 spec). Drop the count constraint; baby handles singleton verifications too. Adjusted rule: baby owns items where review_class === 'verification' OR (review_class is null AND source IN baby-owned-set) OR (orphan assigned-analyst ISS with severity in {minor, moderate}). analyst owns: review_class IN {'deep-attack', 'holistic'} OR (review_class is null AND source NOT in baby-owned-set) OR severity=major/critical orphan ISSs.

by_source.directive_proposal: baby owns: source IN {decider-bau-route, decider-m1-route, decider-m3-carry-over} when review_class='verification'. analyst owns: everything else.
by_source.tinker_evaluation: Approve with relaxation. The 'when review_class=verification' constraint is too restrictive — many decider-routed entries have null review_class (decider doesn't always set it). Adjusted: source IN baby-owned-set with review_class IN {'verification', null} → baby. source IN baby-owned-set with review_class IN {'deep-attack', 'holistic'} → analyst (decider's explicit declaration overrides source default).

the_hybrid_rule_recommended.summary: Primary axis = review_class, fallback axis = source, override = orphan handling.
decision_tree: 1. If review_class IN {'deep-attack', 'holistic'} → ROUTE TO analyst (regardless of source)
2. Else if review_class === 'verification' → ROUTE TO baby
3a. If source IN {'decider-bau-route', 'decider-m1-route', 'decider-m3-carry-over'} → ROUTE TO baby
3b. Else → ROUTE TO analyst (default safe: judgment-heavy)
4. Orphan handling (assigned-analyst ISS not in tracker):
4a. If severity IN {'minor', 'moderate'} → baby (creates a self-authored tracker entry, then processes)
4c. If severity is null → analyst (default safe)

phased_implementation_plan.rationale: The directive's empirical motivation is real but the system is currently net-draining (closure velocity 7d = 151, new velocity = 16, net = +135). The split is architecturally clean and inexpensive, but a full directive-as-stated deployment (Mode 1 + 2b + 1b verification on Sonnet hourly) crosses three boundaries simultaneously: new agent, new scope, new model. Phased deployment lets us validate each boundary independently and roll back any phase if it underperforms.

phase_1.name: Phase 1: Mode 1 BAU drain ONLY, Sonnet, 2h cadence
scope: New scheduled task: dome-analyst-baby, cron `20 */2 * * *`, model claude-sonnet-4-6 (or successor available at deployment time).
New prompt file: monitor/prompts/analyst-baby.md (~50-80 lines, narrow dispatcher).
Baby's dispatcher ONLY runs Mode 1 BAU drain per the hybrid rule decision tree above. All other modes are explicitly delegated to analyst.

Analyst's prompt (analyst.md) is edited to add a Phase-1 cross-reference note: 'baby-analyst handles Mode 1 BAU drain — analyst still handles Mode 1 deep-attack singletons. Both consult the same tracker; routing decided by review_class + source.'
Tinker's pipeline-health check (tinker-pipeline-health.md) is extended to monitor baby's freshness — latest-baby-summary.txt staleness check, every-2h cadence verified.
PROP-030 backlog-trend metrics gain a `baby_drain_count_7d` field (count of EXPs authored by baby in the last 7 days) so we can measure Phase 1 effectiveness.
exit_criteria_phase_to_2: assigned_analyst_count drops from 151 to <50 within 7 days (orphan backlog drained)
OR: 5 consecutive days of net_velocity_7d > 0 with assigned_analyst_count stable or declining
AND: zero baby-vs-analyst write-collision incidents in tinker reports
AND: zero baby-authored EXPs flagged by curmudgeon as inadequate-quality on re-review (verifies Sonnet is producing acceptable BAU work)
rollback_criteria: If baby-authored EXPs are flagged by curmudgeon as inadequate-quality more than 2 in 7 days → disable baby's scheduled task, route work back to analyst.
If tracker write-conflicts exceed 1/day for 3 consecutive days → revisit the claim-stamp mechanism in Q4, possibly add a per-item lock file.

phase_2.name: Phase 2: Add Mode 2b attention-inbox Phase 1+2 (OBE + batch) to baby
phase_2.conditional_on: Phase 1 exit criteria met. NOT before.
scope: Edit analyst-baby.md to add Mode 2b dispatcher entry constrained to Phase 1 OBE + Phase 2 small-item batch (the procedural Phase 3 singleton substantive work stays on analyst).

Reason for the split-within-Mode-2b: attention-inbox Phase 1 (OBE triage) and Phase 2 (batch verification) are explicitly mechanical empirical verification per analyst.md L139-156. Phase 3 (singleton substantive) is judgment-heavy — re-examining a section with fresh eyes.
Phase 1+2 are perfect Sonnet work. Phase 3 stays on Opus analyst.
exit_criteria_phase_to_3: Phase 2 runs for at least 14 days with zero quality regressions
AND: prediction_class field exists on predictions.json (prerequisite for any Mode 1b carve-out)
AND: operator explicitly requests Phase 3 (this is a high-risk phase due to Mode 1b being a state-bearing surface — our_verdict on dome predictions)

phase_3.name: Phase 3 (SPECULATIVE): Mode 1b verification-class predictions to baby
phase_3.conditional_on: Phase 2 success AND prediction_class field exists on predictions.json AND operator explicit approval.
scope: Define `prediction_class` field on predictions.json (NEW schema — needs a separate PROP). Values: `verification` (mechanical: look up a number, check a date, compare an observation against a stated value) vs `deep-analysis` (interpret a pattern, weigh competing hypotheses).
Migrate existing predictions to declared class (analyst Mode 1b authoring writes the class going forward; backfill is operator-side).
Add Mode 1b verification-class entries to baby's dispatcher.
phase_3.rationale_for_speculative_status: The directive proposes this, but the codebase lacks the prerequisite schema. Authoring a Mode 1b prediction is state-bearing (it goes into data/predictions.json and affects our public verdicts). Risk of Sonnet authoring a low-quality verdict before we've validated Phase 1+2 → don't even design Phase 3 in detail until Phase 1+2 land.

estimated_phase_1_implementation_cost.prompt_file_authoring: 1-2 tinker runs to author analyst-baby.md from analyst.md scaffold. Tinker can self-apply under PROP-014-amendment-001 Q6 if operator approves this PROP.
estimated_phase_1_implementation_cost.analyst_md_edits: 30 lines of edits (dispatcher cross-reference + Mode 1 BAU drain delegation note). Tinker self-applies.
estimated_phase_1_implementation_cost.claude_md_update: Extend the Monitoring Pipeline table with the new agent row. Operator-or-tinker.
estimated_phase_1_implementation_cost.tinker_pipeline_health_extension: 5-line addition to the staleness check for latest-baby-summary.txt. Tinker self-applies.
estimated_phase_1_implementation_cost.prop_030_metric_extension: Add `baby_drain_count_7d` field to backlog-trend computation. Tinker self-applies.
estimated_phase_1_implementation_cost.total_estimated_calendar_time_to_phase_1: 1 tinker run + 1 operator review session + 1 scheduled-task creation. ~2 hours total operator+agent attention.

dome_analyst_baby.description: BAU tracker drain + (Phase 2) attention-inbox sweep. High volume, narrow scope. Sonnet for cost + literal rule-compliance.
dome_analyst_baby.model: claude-sonnet-4-6 (or successor available at deployment time)
dome_analyst_baby.rationale_cron_choice: 2h cadence (not hourly per directive). At hourly, 19-22 of 24 daily runs are no-ops in steady state — wasted context-load cost. At 2h, 8-11 of 12 daily runs are no-ops, which is still slack-heavy but better fits the work-arrival rate. :20 offset chosen to avoid collision with analyst at :50, decider at :10, curmudgeon at :30 — provides a 10-30min isolation window from every other agent's run.
outputs: monitor/analyst-baby/expansions/EXP-NNN-baby-*.json (baby-authored expansions — flagged with `authored_by: 'analyst-baby'` field for curmudgeon's re-review attention)
monitor/analyst-baby/issue-proposals/proposal-*.json (for assigned-analyst ISS resolution signaling, same convention as analyst)

dome_analyst_existing.description: Updated description noting the split. Schedule UNCHANGED.
dome_analyst_existing.prompt_file: monitor/prompts/analyst.md (EDITED — Phase 1 dispatcher delegation note for Mode 1 BAU drain; Modes 0, 1 deep-attack, 1b, 2, 2b Phase 3, 3, 4, 5 unchanged)

analyst_baby_md.purpose: Narrow-scope drain agent. Inherits philosophical context (kernel-of-truth, dome architecture) by reference to analyst.md rather than duplicating.
sections_baby_specific: Dispatcher — narrow priority order: Mode 1 BAU drain ONLY (Phase 1) / + Mode 2b Phase 1+2 (Phase 2)
Sonnet-compliance framing: 'process every BAU-eligible item up to budget cap, do not skip on judgment'
Cost engineering reminder: 'this agent is cheap; batch eagerly; if budget reaches 70% of expected, defer remaining to next 2h cycle'
Hybrid-rule decision tree (the §design_space_evaluation.the_hybrid_rule_recommended rule, restated as bash filter)
Escalation protocol: when intake reveals deep-attack scope, write an `escalate-to-analyst.json` marker per Q2 above
Quality discipline: baby's EXPs MUST include `authored_by: 'analyst-baby'` field so curmudgeon's c4 verification cycle attends with appropriate skepticism on the first few runs
analyst_baby_md.estimated_length: 50-80 lines (vs analyst.md's 295 lines). The brevity is intentional — Sonnet performs better on narrow, explicit instructions than on broad judgment-cued prompts.

analyst_md_edits.dispatcher_section_addition: After Mode 1 trigger check, add: '**Note: Mode 1 BAU drain is delegated to dome-analyst-baby (Phase 1, PROP-034). This Opus analyst handles ONLY Mode 1 deep-attack singletons and Mode 1 holistic items. Filter the tracker pending list by `review_class IN {deep-attack, holistic} OR (review_class is null AND source NOT IN baby-owned-set)` before entering Mode 1.**'
analyst_md_edits.mode_1_intake_filter_extension: Add a node block at the top of Mode 1 entry: filter out items that match the baby-owned rule (review_class=verification or source=decider-*-route with null class). Skip those; they're baby's.
analyst_md_edits.mode_2b_phase_1_2_delegation_phase_2_onl: If Phase 2 has landed: add 'Phase 1 OBE and Phase 2 batch are delegated to dome-analyst-baby; this analyst handles ONLY Phase 3 singleton substantive items.' Phase 2 edit is conditional and lands as a separate amendment after Phase 1 exit criteria met.

reference_analyst_mode1_expansions_md_ed.class_hint_intake_section_extension: After the existing class_hint intake table (L89-95), add: 'If `review_class === verification` (declared) OR (`review_class === null` AND source IN baby-owned-set) → this item belongs to dome-analyst-baby. If you (Opus analyst) reach an item in this category, escalate-or-skip — set tracker entry `assigned_to: analyst-baby` and move on. Do not produce a deep-attack EXP for a BAU-drain item.'

reference_data_schemas_md_edits.expansion_tracker_section_extension: Add a note: 'expansion-tracker.json is now read by THREE agents under PROP-034: decider (writer, M1 routing), dome-analyst (reader+writer, Mode 1 deep-attack/holistic + Mode 3 defense), dome-analyst-baby (reader+writer, Mode 1 BAU drain). Multi-writer protection is the same as decider+analyst (PROP-029 git pull --rebase + pre-push integrity gate + non-concurrent scheduling).'
reference_data_schemas_md_edits.tracker_item_field_additions: Optional new fields per item: `claimed_by` ('analyst' | 'analyst-baby' | null), `claimed_at` (ISO timestamp). Set by agent BEFORE work begins; cleared when status flips to 'complete'.

claude_md_edits.monitoring_pipeline_table_extension: Add a row: '| dome-analyst-baby | Every 2h | Sonnet | analyst-baby.md | BAU tracker drain (Phase 1) / + Mode 2b OBE+batch (Phase 2) |'. Phase 2 row-text lands when Phase 2 lands.
claude_md_edits.file_ownership_extensions: Add `monitor/analyst-baby/latest-baby-summary.txt` to workspace-owned. Add `monitor/analyst-baby/expansions/` and `monitor/analyst-baby/issue-proposals/` to append-only directories. The build.js OWNERSHIP and workspace-sync.md OWNED_BY_GIT updates are mechanical.

workspace_sync_md_edits.smart_copy_addition: If baby writes outputs to monitor/analyst-baby/, workspace-sync's normal smart_copy iteration over monitor/ subdirectories already covers it — no special-case code needed. Verify by reading workspace-sync.md to confirm iteration covers all monitor/ subdirs, not a hardcoded list. (Tinker reads this during Phase 1 implementation.)

baseline_today_observed.assigned_analyst_queue_change: +0 net (151 stable; HNOTE-driven drain was zero today)

phase_1_simulation_today_replayed.expected_baby_no_ops: approx 3-5 (no Mode 1 BAU-eligible work after the bolus drains)
phase_1_simulation_today_replayed.iss_drained_via_baby_one_day: approx 30-60 (vs today's 12 via analyst's single BAU drain)
phase_1_simulation_today_replayed.opus_work_today_unchanged: c3/c5 batches + singleton — same deep work as today
phase_1_simulation_today_replayed.assigned_analyst_queue_change: approx -30 to -60 (orphan backlog finally drained at scale)
phase_1_simulation_today_replayed.estimated_sonnet_cost_today: approx $0.30-1.50 (12 runs × $0.05-0.15 per drain run, less for no-op runs at <$0.05)
phase_1_simulation_today_replayed.estimated_total_cost_today_phase_1: approx $5-10 Opus + $0.30-1.50 Sonnet ≈ $5-12. Slight increase from today's $5-10 baseline (the Sonnet $0.30-1.50 is net-new). Modest cost-for-throughput tradeoff.
phase_1_simulation_today_replayed.net_benefit: approx 30-60 ISSs drained/day vs 12 today. Backlog of 137 orphans drains in 3-5 days (vs current trend: never, queue is flat).

phase_1_simulation_steady_state_post_dra.expected_baby_no_ops: approx 8-11 (decider produces only ~5-10 new BAU items/day per PROP-031)
phase_1_simulation_steady_state_post_dra.iss_drained_via_baby_per_day: approx 5-10 (matches decider's PROP-031 production rate — net-zero queue at steady state)
phase_1_simulation_steady_state_post_dra.interpretation: In steady state, baby is over-provisioned but cheap. The cost of having it on call for the rare days when decider produces 20+ BAU items is worth the constant-low cost.

counter_argument.stipulated: Operator explicitly requires this section per directive task_5. Tinker MUST honestly evaluate whether the split is premature/overkill.

case_a_sample_size_is_one_day_could_be_n.argument: Today's 1-of-5 Mode 1 BAU drain dispatch rate is a single data point. The system might fire BAU drain more often on quieter days. We have no baseline data on the multi-day BAU dispatch rate.
case_a_sample_size_is_one_day_could_be_n.counter_evidence: Queue-history.jsonl shows assigned_analyst_count is FLAT at ~151 over the past 36+ hours despite a healthy closure velocity of 135/7d. If BAU drain were firing more often on average, the queue would be shrinking. The flat queue is the structural evidence — independent of today's 4-vs-5 run count.
case_a_sample_size_is_one_day_could_be_n.tinker_verdict: Concern is partially valid but the queue-trend evidence is stronger than the single-day dispatch evidence. Recommendation: log baby-drain-count daily and treat first 7 days as a real measurement; if Phase 1 ends Day 7 with the orphan backlog NOT drained, that's the actual failure mode.

case_b_recent_fixes_need_steady_state_ti.argument: PROP-031 (anti-tap-out) landed 2026-05-11 (2 days ago) and the consolidation language extension landed today. Both increase decider's BAU 3b output rate. Neither has had 5-7 days to demonstrate steady-state. The current backlog drain might be a recovery from the pre-PROP-031 stalled state, not the new steady-state. Adding split-analyst now is changing two variables at once.
case_b_recent_fixes_need_steady_state_ti.counter_evidence: PROP-031 has been live 2 days; we've seen 30+ decider-bau-route entries in that window. The CREATION rate of BAU items is established. What's NOT yet visible is whether analyst's BAU drain can keep up — and today's 1-of-5 dispatch suggests no.
case_b_recent_fixes_need_steady_state_ti.tinker_verdict: Strong concern. Recommendation: phased deployment is exactly the response to this concern. Phase 1 deploys baby in a 'BAU drain only' scope that is reversible (disable scheduled task → drain stops, no data corruption, no architectural change to roll back). If the system would have drained naturally without baby, baby's no-op rate will be high and we'll know to roll back. If baby drains aggressively and queue shrinks, we'll know it was necessary.

case_c_cheaper_alternative_analyst_at_2h.argument: We considered yesterday (2026-05-12 session) that bumping analyst's cadence from 4h to 2h is a much smaller intervention than splitting. At 2h analyst cadence with the SAME dispatcher, BAU drain would fire on ~2-3 of 12 daily runs instead of ~1 of 5. That alone might drain the backlog without architectural complexity.
case_c_cheaper_alternative_analyst_at_2h.counter_evidence: Bumping analyst to 2h cadence doubles Opus costs (~5 runs → ~10 runs/day) without addressing the underlying issue: Opus's dispatcher is judgment-heavy and gravitates toward 'interesting work' (curmudgeon reviews, defender pivots) rather than BAU drain. Even at 2h cadence, Opus might fire BAU drain on 2-3 of 12 runs vs the directive's preferred 12+ baby runs/day. Cost: ~doubled Opus cost (~$10-20/day) vs Sonnet split (~$5-12/day total) — split is cheaper despite adding an agent.
case_c_cheaper_alternative_analyst_at_2h.tinker_verdict: The 2h-cadence alternative is real, cheaper to deploy (one cron edit), and reversible. RECOMMENDATION: as a pre-Phase 1 step, BUMP ANALYST TO 2h CADENCE FOR 3 DAYS. If that alone drains the backlog meaningfully (assigned_analyst_count drops below 100), defer the split until next time we see this pattern. If 3 days of 2h-cadence Opus doesn't drain it (most likely outcome — the dispatcher gravity is the issue, not the cadence), proceed to Phase 1 split. This 'try cheaper first' step is operator-friendly and provides a clean fallback path. NOTE: this contradicts the directive's preferred path; the operator should weigh this counter-recommendation explicitly.

case_d_split_curmudgeon_too.argument: If we split analyst, should we also split curmudgeon? Curmudgeon's batch-gate (PROP-025) already classifies by review_class — same dimension proposed for analyst split. Symmetry argument: do both at once for architectural consistency.
case_d_split_curmudgeon_too.counter_evidence: Curmudgeon's review_class-based batching ALREADY handles its workload: verification reviews batch (3 items × 20KB), deep-attack reviews are singleton (1 review per run). Curmudgeon doesn't have the analyst's 'dispatcher gravity toward interesting work' problem because its dispatcher is queue-driven, not mode-driven. There's no observed bottleneck on curmudgeon. Splitting it would be solution-in-search-of-a-problem.
case_d_split_curmudgeon_too.tinker_verdict: Operator's stated lean ('analyst first, then curmudgeon if same pattern shows up') is correct. Don't split curmudgeon now. Re-evaluate after Phase 1 of analyst split has 14 days of data.

case_e_quality_risk_of_sonnet_on_bau_dra.argument: Sonnet authoring expansions might produce lower-quality work than Opus. The 'kernel of truth' standard that analyst.md emphasizes (find what dome got right, acknowledge it, then show why it fails) is judgment-heavy. Sonnet may flatten this into mechanical bust-the-claim work.
case_e_quality_risk_of_sonnet_on_bau_dra.counter_evidence: BAU drain work is NOT 'find the kernel' work — it's verification close-out: confirming the patch landed cleanly, the citation is correct, the value matches the data file. These are mechanical tasks where Sonnet's literal rule-compliance is a STRENGTH. The deep work (Mode 0 new WIN onboarding, Mode 1 deep-attack singletons) stays on Opus. If Phase 1 reveals Sonnet quality regression on BAU drain, the rollback criteria fire (curmudgeon c4 flags >2 inadequate-quality baby EXPs in 7d → disable baby).
case_e_quality_risk_of_sonnet_on_bau_dra.tinker_verdict: Risk is real but mitigated by phase 1's narrow scope + curmudgeon's c4 re-review safety net. Acceptable.

case_f_writers_to_tracker_collision_comp.argument: Adding a third writer (baby) to the tracker introduces more chances for stale-clone bugs. The decider+analyst dual-writer pattern has already been a source of bugs (CLAUDE.md §FUSE Staleness Warning).
case_f_writers_to_tracker_collision_comp.counter_evidence: The dual-writer bug class is about FUSE↔git sync, not about multiple agents writing to git. Baby's writes go through its own clone like every other agent, with the same git pull --rebase + pre-push integrity gate. Adding a third writer scales the multi-writer protection symmetrically. The structural risk is bounded.
case_f_writers_to_tracker_collision_comp.tinker_verdict: Acceptable. The Q4 claim-stamp mechanism adds belt-and-suspenders protection against the new failure mode (two agents picking up the same item).

counter_argument.overall_counter_argument_synthesis: Three of six concerns (a, b, c) are STRONG enough to warrant the phased deployment. One (c — 'try 2h Opus first') is strong enough to potentially PREEMPT the split entirely if it works. The remaining three (d, e, f) are addressable through the phased plan's exit/rollback criteria. RECOMMENDED PATH: (1) operator bumps analyst to 2h cadence as a 3-day experiment FIRST; (2) if backlog drains, defer this PROP; (3) if backlog doesn't drain, deploy Phase 1 of this PROP. This gives the operator a graceful escalation ladder rather than a binary big-or-nothing decision.

what_NOT_to_do.stipulated: Per directive task_6: explicit 'do not touch' section.
what_NOT_to_do.do_not_redesign_analyst_md_from_scratch: analyst.md is 295 lines of carefully-evolved Opus instructions. Phase 1 edits are LIMITED to: (1) dispatcher cross-reference note delegating Mode 1 BAU drain to baby, (2) Mode 1 intake filter to skip baby-owned items. No restructure, no philosophy changes.
what_NOT_to_do.do_not_change_prop_025_review_class_sema: review_class values remain {'verification', 'deep-attack', 'holistic'} per PROP-025. The split uses these as ROUTING signals, not as authoring guidance. Authors continue to declare review_class per PROP-025 rules.
what_NOT_to_do.do_not_touch_decider_curmudgeon_or_tinke: Decider's M1 routing already writes review_class hint via PROP-029. No edit needed. Curmudgeon's c4 verification continues to attend to all EXPs regardless of authoring agent. Tinker's pipeline-health check is the only tinker-side change — and it's additive.
what_NOT_to_do.do_not_introduce_per_task_model_override: Use cowork's scheduled-task model assignment (the `model` field in the task config). No dynamic model-selection logic inside the prompt files.
what_NOT_to_do.do_not_split_curmudgeon_in_this_prop: Operator's lean: sequence analyst first, watch 14+ days, then re-evaluate curmudgeon. Separate directive if/when warranted.
what_NOT_to_do.do_not_extend_to_mode_0_or_mode_3: New WIN onboarding (Mode 0) and defense neutralization (Mode 3) are judgment-heavy and stay on Opus. These are not in scope for the split now or in any phase.
what_NOT_to_do.do_not_design_phase_3_in_detail_in_this_: Phase 3 (Mode 1b verification-class predictions) requires prediction_class schema work. That's a separate PROP. This PROP describes Phase 3 only at the 'speculative future direction' level, not at implementable-design level.

phase_1_estimated.phase_1_subtotal_steady_state: approx $14-36/day (range overlap with baseline — net cost-neutral within noise)

cost_analysis.phase_1_cost_vs_throughput_summary: Phase 1 adds ~$0.60-2.40/day on top of current baseline. In exchange: 30-60 ISSs drained/day during the first 5 days (~3-5x current), then 5-10 ISSs/day matching decider's BAU production rate. Cost-per-ISS-drained: drops from ~$0.40-1.00 to ~$0.10-0.25 because Sonnet is much cheaper per item.
cost_analysis.rollback_cost: Disabling baby's scheduled task takes 1 minute operator-side. No data migration needed (tracker entries baby created remain valid; their `assigned_to: 'analyst-baby'` field is informational, not blocking).

acceptance_criteria_phase_1: Within 7 days of Phase 1 deploy: assigned_analyst_count drops below 80 (from current 151, target 47% reduction)
AND: zero curmudgeon c4 verification findings of `inadequate-quality` against baby-authored EXPs
AND: zero tracker write-conflicts requiring manual operator intervention
AND: PROP-030 backlog-trend metrics show net_velocity_7d remains positive throughout the window
If all met: declare Phase 1 successful, plan Phase 2.
If assigned_analyst_count drops to <50 (target 67% reduction): consider Phase 2 immediately rather than waiting full 14 days.

monitor/prompts/tinker.md (EDITED — backlog-trend metric extension to include baby_drain_count_7d, ~3 lines added in PROP-030 pre-flight block)
monitor/prompts/workspace-sync.md (VERIFY — confirm smart_copy iteration covers monitor/analyst-baby/ without code change; if hardcoded list, add the directory)

build.js (EDITED — OWNERSHIP object: add monitor/analyst-baby/latest-baby-summary.txt as workspace-owned; add monitor/analyst-baby/expansions/ and issue-proposals/ as append-only — ~5 lines added)
Scheduled task creation: dome-analyst-baby (operator-side, Claude desktop UI or scheduled-tasks MCP)
implementation_authority: PROP authorizes design only. On operator approval:
implementation_authority_steps: Step 1 (operator): Decide whether to take the §counter_argument.case_c 'try 2h Opus first' detour. If yes, defer steps 2-6 by 3 days.
Step 2 (operator): Create dome-analyst-baby scheduled task via Claude desktop UI or scheduled-tasks MCP. Set model=claude-sonnet-4-6, cron=20 */2 * * *, enabled=false initially.

Step 3 (tinker self-apply under PROP-014-amendment-001 Q6 lifecycle): Author analyst-baby.md, edit analyst.md + reference files + CLAUDE.md + build.js + tinker.md per §implementation_files list. Commit + push.
Step 4 (operator): Verify analyst-baby.md content. On approval, set dome-analyst-baby enabled=true.
Step 5 (tinker/operator): Watch first 24h of baby runs — check that latest-baby-summary.txt is fresh, baby's tracker writes are landing, baby is not picking up deep-attack items.
Step 6 (tinker, every run for 7 days): Append baby_drain_count_7d to PROP-030 metrics; check Phase 1 acceptance criteria; report progress in tinker reports.

open_questions_for_operator: Q-OPERATOR-1: Take the §counter_argument.case_c detour (bump analyst to 2h Opus cadence as a 3-day pre-experiment) or proceed directly to Phase 1 split deployment?
Q-OPERATOR-2: Approve Phase 1 scope as designed (Mode 1 BAU drain only on Sonnet 2h), OR prefer a wider Phase 1 (Mode 1 + Mode 2b OBE/batch) at the slightly-higher-risk cost?
Q-OPERATOR-3: 2h cadence (recommended) or hourly cadence (directive-proposed)? My recommendation is 2h with hourly as fallback if 2h proves insufficient.

Q-OPERATOR-4: Auto-rollback behavior on Phase 1 quality regression (curmudgeon flags >2 baby EXPs as inadequate-quality in 7d): disable baby automatically, OR require operator confirmation before disabling?

Q-OPERATOR-5: Tinker self-apply authority — should tinker apply the prompt edits under PROP-014-amendment-001 Q6 lifecycle on PROP approval, or does the operator want to review each prompt-file diff individually?
